title: [FIRRTL] optimize away resetless registers [ConstantProp] [DCE]
body:
The registers in the following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input clock: Clock
    input reset: UInt<1>
    output out_b: UInt<8>
    reg tmp_a: UInt<8>, clock
    reg tmp_b: UInt<8>, clock
    tmp_a <= UInt<8>(5)
    tmp_b <= tmp_a
    out_b <= tmp_b
```

`firrtl-1.5.0` compiler eliminates registers, propagates constant through, produces the following Verilog.

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input        clock,
  input        reset,
  output [7:0] out_b
);
  assign out_b = 8'h5;
endmodule
```

Compiled with `firtool --lower-to-rtl` produces suboptimal Verilog:


```verilog
module top_mod(
  input        clock, reset,
  output [7:0] out_b);

  reg [7:0] tmp_a;	// a_top_mod.fir:6:5
  reg [7:0] tmp_b;	// a_top_mod.fir:7:5

  ....

  always_ff @(posedge clock) begin	// a_top_mod.fir:8:11
    tmp_a <= 8'h5;	// a_top_mod.fir:8:{11,14}
    tmp_b <= tmp_a;	// a_top_mod.fir:9:11
  end // always_ff @(posedge)
  assign out_b = tmp_b;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3
endmodule
```
